story.text: Confirming a brand-new arc could fail confusingly. `cmd_confirm` (the PR pen) spins a worktree off origin/main and calls `confirm_arc`, which validates the epic against the trunk; `--from-ref` (issue #245, done-line 0130) lets it read the epic from the introducing PR's branch instead, so a new arc's first piece can land. But a new arc confirmed with NO `--from-ref` fell through to `confirm_arc` returning None, then an empty `git commit` aborting -- bdo saw a raw 'nothing to commit / not currently on any branch' git error, not the paved path. This hardens that seam: a pure `establish_plan(epic_on_trunk, source_ref)` decision (confirm | establish | refuse) is wired into `cmd_confirm`, so a forward confirm of an unestablished arc with no ref is refused with `--from-ref` named, while the on-trunk and `--from-ref` paths stay byte-unchanged and `--off` (withdrawal) is unaffected. Value: the owner's arc-confirmation seam fails with the paved path, never a raw git error -- the teeth (the value this stakes): a brand-new arc with no definition source is refused, not silently waved through to a confusing failure.

_note: A session+bdo follow-through to the herald landing (2026-06-19): the optional hardening bdo asked to complete. It serves epic.owner-harness (the owner's arc-confirmation flow), already confirmed on the trunk, so it lands via the merge-node with no bootstrap. The value gate judges the story; the value-confirm gate verifies the establish_plan helper + its tests are real on disk. Never mutated -- the fold over .ai-native/log/ is the live state (D-5).
